questions.question: Which of the following elements is classified as an actinoid group element?
Which acid is used in non-ionic detergent reactions to improve cleaning qualities and thicken detergents?
The Contact Process is used for the manufacturing of which of the following chemicals?
In which of the following groups does hydrogen belong?
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of acids?
questions.option_3: They release OH− ions in water.
questions.option_4: They react with bases to form salt and water.

questions.question: Which resilient and adaptable polymer is frequently found in industrial brushes, toothbrushes, and other cleaning supplies?
According to the Brønsted-Lowry theory, which of the following defines an acid?
Which general molecular structure characterises the class of organic compounds known as diazonium compounds or diazonium salts?
Which two chemists are credited with developing the law of mass action in 1864, which states that the concentration of the reactants determines how quickly a chemical change occurs?
questions.option_1: Cato M Guldberg and Peter Waage
questions.option_4: Carl Wilhelm Scheele and Humphry Davy

questions.question: Which method used in metallurgy specifically describes the formation of metal oxides by heating carbonate ores?
Which of the following elements is found in the p-block?
Harold C Urey was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, in 1934:
questions.option_1: for the discovery of heavy hydrogen
questions.option_2: for discovery in the chemistry of the transuranium elements
questions.option_3: for the discovery and development of conductive polymers
questions.option_4: for the development of the density-functional theory

questions.question: What is baking soda chemically known as?
Which postulate of Dalton’s atomic theory reflects the Law of Conservation of Mass?
questions.option_1: Atoms of different elements have different masses and properties.
questions.option_2: Atoms combine in simple whole-number ratios to form compounds.
questions.option_3: Atoms cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction.
questions.option_4: All matter is made up of indivisible particles called atoms.

questions.question: Although a colloid appears homogeneous, it is actually a __________.
Which of the following chemical reactions does NOT represent a balanced equation?
Which of the following is a correct representation of a compound formed by ionic bonding?
Why is an alloy considered a mixture even though it cannot be separated by physical methods?
questions.option_1: It is formed only by non-metals.
questions.option_2: It contains only one type of element in vapor form.
questions.option_3: It shows the properties of its constituents and can have variable composition.
questions.option_4: Alloys are always magnetic because they contain metals with a fixed composition.

questions.question: What name did Dobereiner give to his groups of three similar elements?
Which of the following minerals is known for its insulating properties and is used in electrical industries?
In 1869, who is credited with publishing the first widely recognized version of the periodic table?
Which chemical reaction produces ortho-hydroxybenzoic acid, the primary reaction product used as a raw material for medications?
What is the term used for a substance that reduces the rate of a chemical reaction?
Which of the following elements is classified as a lanthanoid group element?
Why are soft drink and soda water bottles sealed under high pressure in beverage industries?
questions.option_1: To decrease the amount of sugar
questions.option_3: To increase the solubility of carbon dioxide
